title: 02 time sheds whole eras outside tina window
prompt: REFERENCE AUTHORITY — Images 1 and 2 lock Tina, blue hoodie, rear-seat position, car interior, lychee Peelerz, and the intimate bite. The car itself stays ordinary and intact. ONE RULE — Each distant musical tuning note causes the entire neighborhood to shed its current historical era as one complete translucent street-shaped shell, revealing a different era below. FRAME — Cinematic passenger-side view holding Tina's calm profile and gummy in the foreground while the world outside dominates. Above the active street hang three enormous hollow cast-off versions of the exact same block: a translucent 1950s suburb, a present-day strip mall, and an overgrown future, each including ghost-thin roads, utility poles, cars, signs without readable text, and building facades. Beneath those shells the live street is a saturated practical 1970s Japanese- fantasy city with rooftop musicians visibly tuning. Tina's unhurried bite is the still point. VISUAL PROOF — three complete discarded time-shells separated in depth above one current street; Tina sees all eras through one ordinary window. AVOID — peeling the car, time-travel portal, duplicate Tinas, generic multiverse circles, city destruction, clock graphics, collage panels.

resolved_params.system_prompt: Create one extraordinary but coherent cinematic Peelerz commercial styleframe, never a collage, contact sheet, storyboard grid, or design sheet. Ben's brief is an open canvas: favor a bold whole- world creative situation over improving any existing car-peel, parachute, or mango-mecha idea. Preserve only the explicitly declared talent/location authorities. Make the prompt's single impossible rule visible at world scale with one dominant verb, clear spatial organization, and a youthful sense of wonder. The result can be excessive, mythic, psychedelic, deadpan, or impossible, but must remain benign, appetizing, and not preschool-coded. No car opening into petals, no parachute, no robot/mecha armor, no construction-helper crew, no rainbow arch, no generic cheering gummy crowd, no captions, labels, borders, watermarks, gore, weapons, or invented package copy.
